title: Led lighting unit comprising a 3d printed structure and method therefor
abstract: A method of manufacturing a LED lighting unit is provided, in which a base module comprises at least one LED which is offset from a center of the base module. A 3D structure is printed over the base module having a light blocking feature over the center of the base module. A LED lighting unit.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of manufacturing an LED lighting unit, the method comprising the steps of: providing a base module (20), wherein the base module (20) comprises at least one LED element (22), the at least one LED element (22) being offset from a center of the base module (20); and printing a 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c) over the base module (20), wherein the 3D structure includes a light blocking feature (21b) that extends over the center of the base module (20), the light blocking feature (21b) being opaque so that it would block or obscure a light output from a LED element being located at the center of the base module (20). 2. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c) further includes an attachment feature (21a) for mechanical attachment of the 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c) to the base module (20). 3. A method as claimed in claim 2, further comprising the step of providing the base module (20) in a recess (23a) of a base support (23), wherein the printing comprises forming the attachment feature (21a) as an overlap portion which extends on the base support (23) and over at least one edge of the base module (20). 4. A method as claimed in claim 3, wherein the base module (20) comprises a central region and one or more flanges (20a, 20b), and wherein the printing comprises forming the overlap portion over the one or more flanges (20a, 20b). 5. A method as claimed in claim 2, further comprising the step of providing the base module (20) in a recess of a base support, wherein the base support comprises connection pins extending within the recess, wherein the base module (20) has openings corresponding to the connection pins, and wherein the printing comprises forming the attachment feature (21a) as a printed structure over the connection pins. 6. A method as claimed in claim 3, 4 or 5, wherein the printing comprises printing the base support (23), interrupting the printing to locate the base module (20) in the recess (23a), and continuing printing to print the 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c). 7. A method as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the base module (20) comprises an electrical circuit, the electrical circuit comprising a conductor track (25) with an interrupt (26) so that the electrical circuit is incomplete, wherein the step of printing a 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c) over the base module (20) provides a short (28) across the interrupt (260) by using a 3D printing filament arrangement including conducting portions (28), and wherein the conducting portions of the 3D printing filament arrangement complete the electrical circuit of the base module (20). 8. An LED lighting unit, comprising: a base module (20), wherein the base module (20) comprises at least one LED element (22), the at least one LED element (22) being offset from a center of the base module (20); and a printed 3D structure (21a, 21b, 21c) over the base module (20), wherein the 3D structure includes a portion (21b) that extends over the center of the base module (20), the portion (21b) being opaque so that it would block or obscure a light output from a LED element being located at the center of the base module (20). 9. A lighting unit as claimed in claim 8, wherein the 3D structure further includes an optical feature (21c) for processing the light output of the at least one LED. 10. A lighting unit as claimed in claim 8 or 9, wherein the 3D structure further includes an attachment feature (21a) for mechanical attachment of the 3D structure to the base module. 11. A lighting unit as claimed in claim 10, comprising a base support (23) having a recess (23a), wherein the base module is provided in the recess, wherein the attachment feature comprises an overlap portion which extends on the base support and over at least one edge of the base module. 12. A lighting unit as claimed in claim 11, wherein the base module comprises a central region and one or more flanges (20a, 20b), wherein the overlap portion extends over the one or more flanges. 13. A lighting unit as claimed in claim 10, further comprising a base support (23) having a recess (23a), wherein the base module (20) is provided in the recess (23a), and wherein the base support (23) comprises connection pins extending within the recess (23a), the base module (20) having openings corresponding to the connection pins, and the attachment feature comprising a printed structure over the connection pins. 14. A lighting unit as claimed in any one of claims 8 to 13, wherein the base module (20) comprises an electrical circuit, and wherein the 3D printed structure (21a, 21b, 21c) has conducting portions (28) which complete the electrical circuit of the base module (20).
